latin_preview: Ego Patricius peccator rusticissimus et minimus omnium fidelium et contemptibilissimus apud plurimos
english_preview: My name is Patrick. I am a sinner, a simple country person, and the least of all believers. I am looked down upon by many.

latin_preview: patrem habui Calpornium diaconum filium quendam Potiti presbyteri, qui fuit uico bannauem taburniae; uillulam enim prope habuit, ubi ego capturam dedi.
english_preview: My father was Calpornius. He was a deacon; his father was Potitus, a priest, who lived at Bannavem Taburniae. His home was near there, and that is where I was taken prisoner.

latin_preview: Annorum eram tunc fere sedecim.
english_preview: I was about sixteen at the time.

latin_preview: Deum enim uerum ignorabam
english_preview: At that time, I did not know the true God.

latin_preview: et Hiberione in captiuitate adductus sum cum tot milia hominum
english_preview: I was taken into captivity in Ireland, along with thousands of others.

latin_preview: secundum merita nostra, quia a Deo recessimus et praecepta eius non custodiuimus
english_preview: We deserved this, because we had gone away from God, and did not keep his commandments.

latin_preview: et sacerdotibus nostris non oboedientes fuimus, qui nos nostram salutem admonebant
english_preview: We would not listen to our priests, who advised us about how we could be saved.

latin_preview: et Dominus induxit super nos iram animationis suae et dispersit nos in gentibus multis etiam usque ad ultimum terrae
english_preview: The Lord brought his strong anger upon us, and scattered us among many nations even to the ends of the earth.

latin_preview: ubi nunc paruitas mea esse uidetur inter alienigenas
english_preview: It was among foreigners that it was seen how little I was.

latin_preview: et ibi Dominus aperuit sensum incredulitatis meae, ut uel sero rememorarem delicta mea
english_preview: It was there that the Lord opened up my awareness of my lack of faith. Even though it came about late, I recognised my failings.

latin_preview: et ut conuerterem toto corde ad Dominum Deum meum
english_preview: So I turned with all my heart to the Lord my God.

latin_preview: qui respexit humilitatem meam et misertus est adolescentiae et ignorantiae meae
english_preview: and he looked down on my lowliness and had mercy on my youthful ignorance.

latin_preview: et custodiuit me antequam scirem eum et antequam saperem uel distinguerem inter bonum et malum
english_preview: He guarded me before I knew him, and before I came to wisdom and could distinguish between good and evil.

latin_preview: et muniuit me et consolatus est me ut pater filium.
english_preview: He protected me and consoled me as a father does for his son.

latin_preview: Unde autem tacere non possum, neque expedit quidem, tanta beneficia et tantam gratiam quam mihi Dominus praestare dignatus est in terra captiuitatis meae
english_preview: That is why I cannot be silent – nor would it be good to do so – about such great blessings and such a gift that the Lord so kindly bestowed in the land of my captivity.

latin_preview: quia haec est retributio nostra, ut post correptionem uel agnitionem Dei exaltare et confiteri mirabilia eius coram omni natione quae est sub omni caelo.
english_preview: This is how we can repay such blessings, when our lives change and we come to know God, to praise and bear witness to his great wonders before every nation under heaven.

latin_preview: Quia non est alius Deus nec umquam fuit nec ante nec erit post haec praeter Deum Patrem ingenitum, sine principio, a quo est omne principium, omnia tenentem, ut didicimus
english_preview: This is because there is no other God… He is the one who was not begotten, the one without a beginning… this is our teaching.

latin_preview: et huius filium Iesum Christum, quem cum Patre scilicet semper fuisse testamur, ante originem saeculi spiritaliter apud Patrem et inenarrabiliter genitum ante omne principium
english_preview: And his son, Jesus Christ, whom we testify has always been… He was begotten in an indescribable way before every beginning.

latin_preview: et per ipsum facta sunt uisibilia et inuisibilia, hominem factum, morte deuicta in caelis ad Patrem receptum
english_preview: Everything we can see… was made through him. He became a human being; and, having overcome death, was welcomed to the heavens to the Father.

latin_preview: et dedit illi omnem potestatem super omne nomen caelestium et terrestrium et infernorum
english_preview: The Father gave him all power over every being, both heavenly and earthly and beneath the earth.

latin_preview: et omnis lingua confiteatur ei quia Dominus et Deus est Iesus Christus, quem credimus et expectamus aduentum ipsius mox futurum
english_preview: Let every tongue confess that Jesus Christ… is Lord and God.

latin_preview: iudex uiuorum atque mortuorum, qui reddet unicuique secundum facta sua
english_preview: He is judge of the living and of the dead; he rewards every person according to their deeds.

latin_preview: et effudit in nobis habunde Spiritum Sanctum, donum et pignus inmortalitatis, qui facit credentes et oboedientes, ut sint filii Dei et coheredes Christi
english_preview: He has generously poured on us the Holy Spirit, the gift and promise of immortality, who makes believers… to be children of God and co-heirs with Christ.

latin_preview: quem confitemur et adoramus unum Deum in trinitate sacri nominis.
english_preview: This is the one we acknowledge and adore – one God in a trinity of the sacred name.

latin_preview: Ipse enim dixit per prophetam: Inuoca me in die tribulationis tuae et liberabo te et magnificabis me.
english_preview: He said through the prophet: 'Call on me in the day of your distress, and I will set you free, and you will glorify me.'

latin_preview: Et iterum inquit: Opera autem Dei reuelare et confiteri honorificum est.
english_preview: Again he said: 'It is a matter of honour to reveal and tell forth the works of God.'
